findings.title: Implement SSO/SAML Integration
findings.description: Integrate SSO via SAML 2.0 and OIDC for enterprise onboarding to facilitate seamless authentication and improve procurement processes.
assumptions: Enterprise customers require SSO for onboarding.

evidence.excerpt: Enterprise prospects require SSO via SAML 2.0 and OIDC before signing contracts.

recommendations: Integrate with Okta, Azure AD, and OneLogin.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user accesses the platform WHEN they attempt to log in via SSO THEN they should be able to authenticate using SAML 2.0 or OIDC.

findings.title: Enhance Data Isolation for Compliance
findings.description: Implement multi-tenant data isolation methods to meet SOC 2 Type II compliance requirements, moving beyond application-level row filtering.
assumptions: Current data isolation methods are insufficient for compliance.

evidence.excerpt: Security audit flagged that tenant data isolation relies on application-level row filtering.

recommendations: Evaluate and implement proper isolation boundaries.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a multi-tenant environment WHEN a security audit is conducted THEN data isolation must be verified at the schema or database level.

findings.title: Implement Fine-Grained Role-Based Access Control
findings.description: Develop a fine-grained RBAC system that allows for custom role definitions, permission inheritance, and resource-level access controls.
assumptions: Current RBAC model is too simplistic.

evidence.excerpt: Enterprise customers need fine-grained RBAC beyond our current admin/member/viewer model.

recommendations: Implement custom permission sets and audit logging.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user with a custom role WHEN they access resources THEN they should only see resources they have permissions for.

findings.title: Implement Data Residency Controls
findings.description: Enable data residency controls to allow enterprise customers to choose where their data is stored, ensuring compliance with GDPR and local laws.
assumptions: Data residency is a barrier to adoption in regulated markets.

evidence.excerpt: Enterprise customers must choose where their data is stored.

recommendations: Implement region-pinned storage with cross-region replication disabled.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a customer selects a data residency option WHEN data is stored THEN it must be stored in the selected region.

findings.title: Implement White-Label Theming and Custom Domain Support
findings.description: Develop white-label branding capabilities allowing enterprise customers to customize logos, color schemes, and domain names.
assumptions: Current architecture does not support white-labeling.

evidence.excerpt: Several enterprise prospects have expressed a need for white-label branding capabilities.

recommendations: Enable custom domain support and branding options.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a customer configures white-label settings WHEN they access the platform THEN the branding should reflect their customizations.

findings.title: Collect Customer Feedback on Features
findings.description: Establish a structured mechanism for collecting customer feedback on feature usage and satisfaction to inform product improvements.
assumptions: Current feedback collection methods are insufficient.

evidence.excerpt: There is a gap in collecting structured feedback from customers regarding feature usage and satisfaction.

recommendations: Implement surveys and feedback tools within the platform.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user accesses the feedback tool WHEN they submit feedback THEN it should be recorded and categorized for analysis.

findings.title: Monitor and Alert on Uptime Metrics
findings.description: Implement monitoring and alerting for uptime metrics to ensure high availability and customer satisfaction.
assumptions: High uptime is critical for customer trust.

evidence.excerpt: Uptime is a guardrail metric that measures the availability of the platform.

recommendations: Set up monitoring tools and alert systems for uptime.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a monitoring tool is in place WHEN uptime drops below 99.9% THEN an alert should be triggered.

findings.title: Conduct Churn Reduction Experiments
findings.description: Run experiments to test various churn reduction strategies and measure their impact on customer retention.
assumptions: Churn reduction strategies can improve retention.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct experiments to test various churn reduction strategies.

recommendations: Implement enhanced onboarding processes and customer engagement initiatives.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a churn reduction strategy is implemented WHEN measuring churn rate THEN the rate should decrease by at least 10% over the next quarter.

findings.title: Implement User Feedback Collection Mechanism
findings.description: Create a mechanism for users to provide feedback on their experience and feature requests directly through the platform.
assumptions: User feedback is essential for product improvement.

evidence.excerpt: There is a gap in collecting structured feedback from customers.

recommendations: Integrate feedback forms and surveys within the application.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user accesses the feedback form WHEN they submit feedback THEN it should be logged for review.

findings.title: Implement Security Audit Logging
findings.description: Create an audit logging mechanism to track permission changes and access control modifications for compliance and security purposes.
assumptions: Audit logging is necessary for compliance.

evidence.excerpt: Requirements include audit logging of all permission changes.

recommendations: Implement a logging system that captures all access control changes.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a permission change occurs WHEN the change is made THEN it should be logged with user details and timestamp.

findings.title: Implement Custom Role Definitions
findings.description: Allow enterprise customers to define custom roles with specific permissions tailored to their organizational needs.
assumptions: Custom roles will enhance user management.

evidence.excerpt: Enterprise customers need fine-grained RBAC beyond our current admin/member/viewer model.

recommendations: Develop a user interface for custom role creation.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN an admin creates a custom role WHEN they define permissions THEN the role should be saved and available for assignment.

findings.title: Implement Permission Inheritance
findings.description: Develop a system for permission inheritance that allows roles to inherit permissions from other roles, simplifying management.
assumptions: Permission inheritance will streamline role management.
recommendations: Implement a hierarchy for role permissions.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a role inherits permissions from another role WHEN the parent role's permissions change THEN the child role's permissions should update accordingly.

summary: The analysis has led to the creation of a comprehensive backlog of requirements focused on enhancing authentication, compliance, access control, and user feedback mechanisms. Each requirement is structured with clear acceptance criteria and dependencies, ensuring a well-defined path for development and prioritization.
